name: Hugin's Eye
description: Hugin's Eye refers to one of the eyes of Hugin, one of Odin's ravens. Odin, the Allfather, possessed two ravens, Hugin (Thought) and Munin (Memory), who flew around the world of Midgard, gathering information and whispering it into Odin's ear. To gain this knowledge, Odin sacrificed one of Hugin's eyes, granting the raven unparalleled insight and the ability to perceive events across vast distances and beyond the veil of the present. The loss of the eye enhanced Hugin's already keen senses, turning it into a focal point for Odin's all-seeing awareness. The eye itself is not a physical object that can be held, but rather a symbolic representation of the price of knowledge and wisdom. It underscores Odin's willingness to sacrifice a part of himself to obtain the intelligence necessary to rule and protect Asgard and the realms beyond. It represents not just sight, but profound understanding and foresight, a connection to the very fabric of existence. The sacrifice imbued Hugin with an almost preternatural awareness, making it an essential part of Odin's intelligence network. While the physical eye is gone, its essence remains within Hugin, and by extension, within Odin's own perception. It is a constant reminder of the cost of wisdom and the burden of knowledge.
subtitle: A Raven's Eye, Odin's Wisdom
origin: Sacrificed by Odin to enhance Hugin's abilities.
currentLocation: Abstractly within Hugin and Odin's awareness.

keyMyths.title: Odin's Ravens
keyMyths.description: Hugin and Munin fly around Midgard and report back to Odin. Hugin's enhanced sight, gained through the sacrifice of his eye, allows him to gather more detailed and accurate information.

keyMyths.title: The Cost of Wisdom
keyMyths.description: Odin's sacrifice of Hugin's eye highlights the theme that true wisdom comes at a price. This sacrifice allows Odin to gain a deeper understanding of the world and its workings.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: Provides detailed accounts of Norse mythology, including Odin and his ravens.
sources.text: Provides detailed accounts of Norse mythology, including Odin and his ravens.

sources.author: Unknown
